predicted_sql: SELECT e.LIGHT_CONDITIONS, e.WEATHER_CONDITIONS, SUM(f.NUMBER_OF_CASUALTIES) AS total_casualties FROM DIM_ENVIRONMENT e JOIN FACT_ACCIDENT f ON e.ENVIRONMENT_ID = f.ENVIRONMENT_ID GROUP BY e.LIGHT_CONDITIONS, e.WEATHER_CONDITIONS HAVING SUM(f.NUMBER_OF_CASUALTIES) > 2 ORDER BY total_casualties DESC;
raw_output: SELECT e.LIGHT_CONDITIONS, e.WEATHER_CONDITIONS, SUM(f.NUMBER_OF_CASUALTIES) AS total_casualties FROM DIM_ENVIRONMENT e JOIN FACT_ACCIDENT f ON e.ENVIRONMENT_ID = f.ENVIRONMENT_ID GROUP BY e.LIGHT_CONDITIONS, e.WEATHER_CONDITIONS HAVING SUM(f.NUMBER_OF_CASUALTIES) > 2 ORDER BY total_casualties DESC;

question: Joh accidents Friday ko hua hain, unke liye har area ke liye list karo jahan accident hua tha, saath mein average number of casualties aur har area ke liye total count of accident IDs.
gold_sql: SELECT T2.AREA_ACCIDENT_OCCURRED, AVG(T1.NUMBER_OF_CASUALTIES), COUNT(T1.ACCIDENT_ID) FROM FACT_ACCIDENT AS T1 JOIN DIM_GEOGRAPHY AS T2 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T2.GEOGRAPHY_ID JOIN DIM_TIME AS T3 ON T1.TIME_ID = T3.TIME_ID WHERE T3.DAY_OF_WEEK = 'Friday' GROUP BY T2.AREA_ACCIDENT_OCCURRED
predicted_sql: SELECT g.area_accident_occurred, AVG(f.number_of_casualties) AS average_casualties, COUNT(DISTINCT f.accident_id) AS total_accident_count FROM fact_accident f JOIN dim_time t ON f.time_id = t.time_id JOIN dim_geography g ON f.geography_id = g.geography_id WHERE t.day_of_week ILIKE 'Friday' GROUP BY g.area_accident_occurred;
raw_output: SELECT g.area_accident_occurred, AVG(f.number_of_casualties) AS average_casualties, COUNT(DISTINCT f.accident_id) AS total_accident_count FROM fact_accident f JOIN dim_time t ON f.time_id = t.time_id JOIN dim_geography g ON f.geography_id = g.geography_id WHERE t.day_of_week ILIKE 'Friday' GROUP BY g.area_accident_occurred;

question: Driver ID, driver ka age band, aur driver ka sex dikhao, saath mein har ek driver ke liye total accidents ka count bhi, specifically un drivers ke liye jinke paas 1-2 years ka driving experience hai ya jo 'Junior high school' level ki education rakhte hain, aur jo zero accidents wale drivers bhi shamil hain, aur results ko accident count ke basis par descending order mein arrange karo.
gold_sql: SELECT T1.DRIVER_ID, T1.AGE_BAND_OF_DRIVER, T1.SEX_OF_DRIVER, COUNT(T2.ACCIDENT_ID) FROM DIM_DRIVER AS T1 LEFT JOIN FACT_ACCIDENT AS T2 ON T1.DRIVER_ID = T2.DRIVER_ID LEFT JOIN BRIDGE_ACCIDENT_CASUALTY AS T3 ON T2.ACCIDENT_ID = T3.ACCIDENT_ID WHERE T1.DRIVING_EXPERIENCE = '1-2yr' OR T1.EDUCATIONAL_LEVEL = 'Junior high school' GROUP BY T1.DRIVER_ID, T1.AGE_BAND_OF_DRIVER, T1.SEX_OF_DRIVER HAVING COUNT(T2.ACCIDENT_ID) >= 0 ORDER BY COUNT(T2.ACCIDENT_ID) DESC
predicted_sql: SELECT d.DRIVER_ID, d.AGE_BAND_OF_DRIVER, d.SEX_OF_DRIVER, COUNT(f.ACCIDENT_ID) AS total_accidents FROM DIM_DRIVER d LEFT JOIN FACT_ACCIDENT f ON d.DRIVER_ID = f.DRIVER_ID WHERE d.DRIVING_EXPERIENCE = '1-2yr' OR d.EDUCATIONAL_LEVEL = 'Junior high school' GROUP BY d.DRIVER_ID, d.AGE_BAND_OF_DRIVER, d.SEX_OF_DRIVER ORDER BY total_accidents DESC;
raw_output: SELECT d.DRIVER_ID, d.AGE_BAND_OF_DRIVER, d.SEX_OF_DRIVER, COUNT(f.ACCIDENT_ID) AS total_accidents FROM DIM_DRIVER d LEFT JOIN FACT_ACCIDENT f ON d.DRIVER_ID = f.DRIVER_ID WHERE d.DRIVING_EXPERIENCE = '1-2yr' OR d.EDUCATIONAL_LEVEL = 'Junior high school' GROUP BY d.DRIVER_ID, d.AGE_BAND_OF_DRIVER, d.SEX_OF_DRIVER ORDER BY total_accidents DESC;

question: Male drivers jo '31-50' ya '18-30' age bands mein hain, unke liye unka educational level aur driving experience dikhao saath mein average number of casualties per accident bhi. Lekin sirf un groups ko include karna jo ek se zyada accidents wale hain. Results ko average casualty count ke basis par descending order mein arrange karna.
gold_sql: SELECT T1.EDUCATIONAL_LEVEL, T1.DRIVING_EXPERIENCE, AVG(T2.NUMBER_OF_CASUALTIES) FROM DIM_DRIVER AS T1 JOIN FACT_ACCIDENT AS T2 ON T1.DRIVER_ID = T2.DRIVER_ID JOIN BRIDGE_ACCIDENT_CASUALTY AS T3 ON T2.ACCIDENT_ID = T3.ACCIDENT_ID WHERE T1.AGE_BAND_OF_DRIVER IN ('31-50', '18-30') AND T1.SEX_OF_DRIVER = 'Male' GROUP BY T1.EDUCATIONAL_LEVEL, T1.DRIVING_EXPERIENCE HAVING COUNT(T2.ACCIDENT_ID) > 1 ORDER BY AVG(T2.NUMBER_OF_CASUALTIES) DESC;
predicted_sql:
SELECT 
    dim_driver.educational_level,
    dim_driver.driving_experience,
    AVG(fact_accident.number_of_casualties) as average_casualties_per_accident
FROM dim_driver
JOIN fact_accident ON dim_driver.driver_id = fact_accident.driver_id
WHERE dim_driver.sex_of_driver = 'Male'
  AND dim_driver.age_band_of_driver IN ('31-50', '18-30')
GROUP BY dim_driver.educational_level, dim_driver.driving_experience
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT fact_accident.accident_id) > 1
ORDER BY AVG(fact_accident.number_of_casualties) DESC;
raw_output:
SELECT 
    dim_driver.educational_level,
    dim_driver.driving_experience,
    AVG(fact_accident.number_of_casualties) as average_casualties_per_accident
FROM dim_driver
JOIN fact_accident ON dim_driver.driver_id = fact_accident.driver_id
WHERE dim_driver.sex_of_driver = 'Male'
  AND dim_driver.age_band_of_driver IN ('31-50', '18-30')
GROUP BY dim_driver.educational_level, dim_driver.driving_experience
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT fact_accident.accident_id) > 1
ORDER BY AVG(fact_accident.number_of_casualties) DESC;
